strategy: Bring a full team. The Titan has 500,000 health and every spell he casts deals 9,999 damage for 14 pips.
Do not stress over survival. If anyone drops low, the fight script casts Dryad and restores them to full health.
Supports blade the hitter every round while the Paradox ally chips in with its free 0-pip spells. The opening aura grants mastery of all seven schools, so everyone's power pips count for any school.
Do not have henchmen cast globals; the script removes them. Henchmen do get seven power pips per round though, so they hit hard here.

Grind the Titan down toward 15% of his health. He and the Paradox wizard gain seven power pips every round, so expect a 9,999-damage spell from him almost every round and keep your own damage constant.
At about 15% he heals back to 100% and you are invisibly polymorphed. That is the win signal, not a setback: cast the granted Scion of Elements or Scion of Spirits to finish him.
cheatNotes: You fight alongside the Divine Paradox, who is locked in a Star polymorph with a fixed set of spells for the whole duel.
The opening aura gives the Paradox wizard mastery of all seven schools, so power pips work for any school's spells all fight.
The boss and the Paradox wizard each gain seven power pips every round. He can afford his 14-pip, 9,999-damage spells nearly every round.
Henchmen you summon also gain seven power pips per round, which makes them unusually strong in this duel.
Globals cast by henchmen are removed. Do not count on henchmen for bubbles.
If you start low or drop low, the script casts Dryad and restores you to full health. Deaths are largely prevented, so do not panic-heal.
At about 15% health the Titan heals back to 100% and invisibly polymorphs you for 99 rounds. This is the finisher phase: cast Scion of Elements or Scion of Spirits to end the fight.
